description: SCOR - About. Descriptor galaxy for the SCOR MISP Framing, the rich knowledge companion to the METEORSTORM MISP taxonomy. METEORSTORM is the only published threat-model framework whose Primary Capability Environment layer captures all five operational environments in a single taxonomy: Terrestrial, Aquatic, Aerial, Orbital, and Deep Space. Every other major catalog covers a slice; METEORSTORM unifies all five under one data model so a SCOR Platform Professional reasoning about a single converged platform does not have to translate between four or five disconnected vocabularies. SCOR is the rich knowledge layer that attaches to METEORSTORM tags, and it normalizes equivalent controls and TTPs from thirteen external frameworks into the Analytic Layer: MITRE ATT&CK, MITRE FiGHT, MITRE ATLAS, MITRE D3FEND, MITRE EMB3D, MITRE CAPEC, Aerospace SPARTA, ESA SPACE-SHIELD, CSA AI Controls Matrix (AICM), CSA Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM), CSA Shared Security Responsibility Model (SSRM), NIST SP 800-160 Volumes 1 and 2, NIST SP 800-53. Each scor-resilience-measures entry carries a framework-mappings metadata field that records the equivalent control in each of these catalogs, so a single SCOR measure becomes the common reference point for controls expressed in many vocabularies. The Framing is built for Space, Cybersecurity, Operations, and Resilience Platform Professionals (SCOR Platform Professionals), the analysts, operators, and engineers defending converged terrestrial, aquatic, aerial, orbital, and deep-space platforms in real time. The Framing consists of six galaxies in two tiers. Four reference galaxies hold the controlled vocabulary inherited from the METEORSTORM data model: scor-tens (the 30 Taxonomic Element Nomenclatures across the five layers PCE/SEG/SVC/AST/AN), scor-exposure-domain (the five exposure domains of METEORSTORM Quick Guide Table 2.2), scor-detection-signatures (AN-DET vocabulary in RootA YAML with mandatory PCE/SEG/SVC/AST layer mapping), and scor-resilience-measures (AN-RES vocabulary with the cross-framework mappings described above). Two informative galaxies illustrate the model in use: scor-incidents (taxonomic illustrations of disclosed incidents, with confidence score and basis) and scor-attack-paths (paths recording initial access, lateral movement, and intended target). Vocabulary distinction: a TEN (Taxonomic Element Nomenclature) is a stable LAYER-TAG-LABEL template (for example SEG-GR-Ground); an ETEN is what a SCOR Platform Professional produces by enumerating that TEN on a specific platform, appending an ordinal in LAYER:TAG:LABEL:ORDINAL form (for example SEG:GR:Ground:01 for the primary ground control operator workstation). Cluster files ship vocabulary and metadata only; relationships among clusters use the six SCOR relation types defined in the Framing reference document Section 8 (TOE, TDM, TRE, exposure-domain, detected-by, mitigated-by) and are built by SCOR Platform Professionals in MISP at investigation time. One structural exception: incidents and attack paths carry TOE related[] links back to the TENs they instantiate, with the per-scenario ETEN string in the relation tags as reference anchoring to the TEN galaxy. Reference-galaxy UUIDs are immutable forever once published; informative galaxies grow under reviewed release. Stewardship: H4CK32N4U75® within the SCORP2 community. Closed-commit governance: only SCORP2 community members commit. SCOR Incidents and SCOR Attack Paths are reviewed and published by certified SCOR Platform Professionals within the SCORP2 community. Public contributions accepted through the published intake channel with attribution. License: CC BY 4.0. Contact: william.o.ferguson@hkn.space. Reference: https://misp-galaxy.org/scor-about/. Note: descriptor only, the matching cluster file provides the Framing overview entries; the six real galaxies provide the working vocabulary.
name: SCOR - About
